reason: Standalone 'meth' is the canonical token for methamphetamine-contamination queries (s49A). Probe 'method'/'methodology' was the original false-match motivating PR1; boundary-aware allow_section() now rejects them. Pinned by test_nz_tenancy_routes.cpp LP boundary regression.
Standalone 'harm' is intentional for s55AA family-violence gating. Probes 'pharmacy'/'charm'/'harmless'/'harmful'/'harmony' boundary-rejected; pinned by LP boundary regression test.
Standalone 'hit' as the violence verb is intentional for s55AA. Boundary check prevents any in-word false-match; common false-positive substrings are non-tenancy.
Standalone 'hurt' (past + present) is intentional for s55AA. No realistic non-boundary false-match in tenancy English.

Bare 'repaint' deliberately excludes wear-and-tear queries from the s49B insurance-excess route. Probes 'repainting'/'repainted' would only false-match under raw substring; boundary check rejects them.
NZ government agency abbreviation (Work and Income New Zealand). Domain-specific; no false-match risk.
Abbreviation for Short Message Service (text). Domain-specific; no false-match risk.
Standalone 'viewing' is the real exclusion target (property sale viewings, listing photos). Probe 'reviewing' would only false-match under raw substring; boundary-aware AC prevents it.
Abbreviation for Heat Recovery Ventilation. Domain-specific; normalize_query lowercases to 'hrv'.
Abbreviation for Healthy Homes Standards. Domain-specific.
NZ agency abbreviation (Housing New Zealand). Domain-specific.
Same rationale as repair_notice_s56: bare 'bond' is the exclusion target; inflected probes are boundary-rejected.
Standalone noun. 'Offence'/'offense' probe: 'fence' appears at position 2 in 'offence' with 'f' as left char (word char) - boundary check FAILS, so no false match. Safe.
Probe 'fixtures' is just the plural. The singular and plural are the same concept; the probe match is a false positive.
Standalone garden noun. No common-word false-match risk.
Standalone noun. 'Lawns' is just the plural; boundary check handles it.
Standalone noun for garden plants. 'Transplant' probe fails boundary check in normal tenancy context.
Standalone garden noun. No common-word false-match risk.
Standalone noun. No realistic common-word confusion in tenancy queries.
Plural of 'tree'. Standalone noun in tenancy context.

Python-side holdover of the pre-PR1 term (C++ removed it post-PR1). Safe under boundary-aware matcher since 'alter' no longer matches inside 'alternative'. Retained in Python precise for parity with older route queries.
Core legal term for tenant modifications (s42A). Plural 'alterations' is the same concept. Probe match is a false positive.
Domain-specific abbreviation for closed-circuit television. Not a common word fragment.
Core legal term for property modifications. Plural probe 'alterations' is the same concept; false-positive probe match.
Same rationale as repair_notice_s56: bare 'bond' is the exclusion target; inflected probes are boundary-rejected.
Standalone 'bond' excludes bond-refund queries from the s56 repair-notice route. Probes 'bonded'/'bonding' rejected by boundary check; bare token is the legitimate exclusion target.
Probe 'alterations' is just the plural. Excluding 'alteration' correctly suppresses property-change queries from this maintenance route. The risk is plural-form, not a different-meaning word.
Acronym for Code of Compliance Certificate. Case-specific; normalize_query lowercases so this matches 'ccc' - an uncommon sequence in non-building contexts.
Pest/insect term. No realistic non-boundary false-match in tenancy context.
Plural of 'bee'. Same rationale.
Pest term (insect). 'Debugging' probe: left char 'e' is word char - boundary fails. Safe after PR1.
Plural of 'bug'. Same rationale.
Core property condition term. 'Dampening' probe: 'damp' at pos 0-3, right char 'e' is word char - boundary fails. Safe after PR1.
Appliance term. No common-word false-match risk.
Pest infestation term. No common-word false-match risk.
Toilet/plumbing term. 'Blushing' probe: left char 'b' is word char - boundary fails. Safe after PR1.
UK/NZ term for cooktop. No common-word false-match risk.
Property condition descriptor. 'Humidity' probe: right char 'i' is word char - boundary fails. Safe after PR1.
Core maintenance term for water ingress. 'Leaking'/'leaks' explicitly added. No false-match risk in tenancy context.
Plural/verb form of 'leak'. Added in PR2 to cover boundary-safe inflections.
Plural of 'mouse' (pest). No common-word confusion.
US spelling variant of 'mould'. Same rationale.
Core NZ tenancy term for mould/dampness issues. 'Remoulding' probe fails boundary check (left 'e' is word char).
Pest term. 'Mousetrap' probe: right char 't' is word char - boundary fails. Safe after PR1.
Kitchen appliance term. 'Coven' probe: left char 'c' is word char - boundary fails. Safe after PR1.
Pest infestation term. No common-word confusion in tenancy context.
Plural of 'pest'. Same rationale.
Plumbing term. No common-word false-match risk.
Pest infestation term. 'Frustrated'/'separate'/'operates' probes: all have word chars as boundary chars. Safe after PR1. This is the canonical example from the boundary fix audit.
Plural of 'rat'. Same rationale.
Kitchen appliance term. No realistic false-match risk.
Pest term. No common English words contain 'wasp' as non-boundary substring.
Plural of 'wasp'. Same rationale.
Common repair verb. 'Fixture'/'prefix'/'affix' probes: all have word chars as left/right boundaries - no false match under boundary checker. Essential term for this route.
Past tense of 'fix'. No common-word false-match risk in tenancy context.
Third-person singular of 'fix'. No common-word false-match risk.
Primary party in NZ tenancy law. Probe 'landlords' is just the plural; same concept.
Standalone noun for door/window locks. 'Clockwork'/'flocking'/'unlock' probes: 'lock' appears with 'c', 'f', 'n' as left chars (all word chars) - boundary check fails in all cases. Safe after PR1.
Verb form. 'Flocking' probe: 'l','o','c','k','i','n','g' in 'flocking' starts at pos 2; left char 'f' is word char - boundary fails. Safe after PR1.
Plural of 'lock'. Same rationale as 'lock'.
Standalone kitchen appliance noun. 'Coven' probe: left boundary is 'c' (word char) so no false match even under raw-substring.
Standard NZ tenancy abbreviation for 'property manager'. Two-letter term safe in context gate only. Boundary-aware matcher handles 'rpm', 'camphor', etc.
Standalone kitchen appliance noun. No realistic false-match risk in tenancy context.
Root form of 'evict'. 'Evicted'/'evicting'/'eviction' added separately in PR2. No false-match risk in tenancy context.
Standalone noun for surface damage. 'Cracking' and 'cracks' added separately. No realistic confusion with drug slang in tenancy context.
Probe match 'repainting'/'repainted' are just inflected forms of the same verb. Semantically identical to the route term; the match is intentional.
Standalone adjective for damaged/degraded material. Appears only at word boundaries in tenancy context.
